A remote code execution vulnerability in libhevc in Mediaserver could enable an attacker using a specially crafted file to cause memory corruption during media file and data processing. This issue is rated as Critical due to the possibility of remote code execution within the context of the Mediaserver process. Product: Android. Versions: 5.0.2, 5.1.1, 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. The prctl_set_vma_anon_name function in kernel/sys.c in Android before 5.1.1 LMY49F and 6.0 before 2016-01-01 does not ensure that only one vma is accessed in a certain update action, which allows attackers to gain privileges or cause a denial of service (vma list corruption) via a crafted application, aka internal bug 20017123.

A remote denial of service vulnerability in libhevc in Mediaserver could enable an attacker to use a specially crafted file to cause a device hang or reboot. This issue is rated as High severity due to the possibility of remote denial of service. Product: Android. Versions: 5.0.2, 5.1.1, 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. Android ID: A-34064500. A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). User interaction is not needed for exploitation. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-111274046. Product: Android Versions: Android-9.0 Android ID: A-111274046. Product: Android Versions: Android-10.0 Android ID: A-111274046. Product: Android Versions: Android

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as Critical due to the possibility of remote attackers conducting tapjacking attacks and accessing private files without a valid access token. Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 should not be used for devices that need to protect against tapjacking attacks, e.g., medical devices, unless the developers have taken steps to ensure that the window sizes do not overlap. Android users should check for updates in the Android system software and, if available, install them. Android developers should ensure that the Android user-interface permission-dialog implementation does not allow for partial overlap of a window with the area that contains the contents of a private-storage file.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as Critical due to the possibility of remote attackers conducting tapjacking attacks and accessing private files without a valid access permission. Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 should not be used for devices that need to protect against tapjacking attacks, e.g., medical devices, cars, and smart TVs. Android developers are encouraged to use Android IDeaOdette (a public version of Android's internal IDeaOdette framework) for permission-dialog implementation to ensure that overlapping windows do not occur. Android IDeaOdette is available from the Android Open Source Project (AOSP) download page. User interaction is not needed to resolve this issue. Product: Android. Versions: Android-6

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as Critical because it can be used to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files without the owner's knowledge. Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 should use the Space-Conserving Double-Buffer approach for permission-dialog windows instead of the default approach, aka internal bug 26677798. Android users should not install unofficial Android apps that could contain malware. Android users should consider setting a passcode to prevent unauthorized access to their device if they do not already do so.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is also known as "extra content in permission-dialog window." Note that this issue does not affect the Android 6.x devices that have obtained the "Official Update" for Android 6.x before 2016-06-01, aka Android ID 162640704. Android ID 162640704 is a unique device identifier that is used for tracking purposes. Android ID 162640704 is not available to the general public.

Android 6.x after 2016-06-01 addresses this issue but introduces the issue of simultaneous tap and drag operation, aka external bug 6007796. Android IDEA products are Android devices that run the Android IDEA user interface configuration engine. The encode_share_access function in fs/nfs/nfs4xdr.c in the Linux kernel before 2.6.29 allows local users to cause a denial of service (BUG and system crash) by using the mknod system call with a pathname on an NFSv4 filesystem.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2008-5773.  Steps for exploitation: (1) use the mknod system call with a pathname on an NFSv4 filesystem, (2) provide an invalid username or hostname, and (3) provide an invalid share access flag.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2008-5773.  Product: Linux. Versions: 2.6.29 through 4.14.4. Linux Kernel Version: 2.6.29 through 4.14.4. NFSv4 Versions: 4.1

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). User interaction is not needed for exploitation. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-111274046. The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as Critical due to the possibility of remote attackers bypassing intended access restrictions via a crafted application, which could lead to local data loss or disclosure. Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 should not be used for devices that require a high level of access restriction protection, such as medical devices or smart TVs. Android users should update to the latest version of Android to remove this vulnerability.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as Critical due to the possibility of remote attackers conducting tapjacking attacks and accessing private files without a valid access permission. Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 should not be used for devices that need to protect against tapjacking attacks, such as medical devices and smart cameras. User interaction is needed for exploitation, aka internal bug 26677793. Android developers are encouraged to use the updated permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x after 2016-06-01.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as Critical because it can be used to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files without the owner's knowledge. Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 should use the Space-Conserving Design Pattern for UI permissions dialogs to prevent this issue. Android 7.0 Android 7.1.1 Android 7.1.2 Android 8.0 Android 8.1 Android 9.0 Android ID Android ID Enterprise does not have this issue.
